DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLLOWS: DEFENDANTS GP JOULE KONZEPT GMBH & CO KG (GP JOULE) APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. SUBSECTIONS (A) AND (C) OF DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER ARE GRANTED AND SUBSECTIONS (B) AND (D) ARE DENIED. SUBSECTIONS (A) AND (C) FLOW FROM THE RIGHTS GRANTED TO DEFENDANT IN ARTICLE 7 OF THE PARTIES' CONTRACT. SUBSECTIONS (B) AND (D) GO BEYOND THE TERMS OF THE PARTIES' CONTRACT. FRONTIER MAY SELL OR ENCUMBER THE "COLLATERAL" OR A PORTION THEROF, IF THE PROCEEDS OF THOSE TRANSACTIONS EXCEED THE VALUE OF THE LOAN PLUS INTEREST, AND THE LOAN PLUS INTEREST WILL BE REPAID IN FULL AS A CONDITION OF THE CLOSE OF THOSE TRANSACTIONS. THE FOREGOING IS DEPENDENT ON DEFENDANT POSTING A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $100,000 NO LATER THAN MARCH 23, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. CROSS DEFENDANT FRONTIER RENEWABLES LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Off calendar. A dismissal of the entire action was filed April 10, 2012. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Ntc Of Application And Application For Preliminary Injunction; Memo Of P & A; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
Off Calendar Mar-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HARMEET K. DHILLON OF DHILLON & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 433-1700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SETH D. HILTON OF STOEL RIVES LLP, TEL. NO. 617-8943 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLLOWS: DEFENDANTS GP JOULE KONZEPT GMBH & CO KG (GP JOULE) Application For Preliminary Injunction IS Granted. Subsections (a) and (c) of defendant's proposed order are granted and subsections (b) and (d) are denied. Subsections (a) and (c) flow from the rights granted to defendant in article 7 of the parties' contract. Subsections (b) and (d) go beyond the terms of the parties' contract. Frontier may sell or encumber the "collateral" or a portion therof, if the proceeds of those transactions exceed the value of the loan plus interest, and the loan plus interest will be repaid in full as a condition of the close of those transactions. The foregoing is dependent on defendant posting a bond in the amount of $100,000 no later than March 23, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
